title: Live-streaming of court proceedings - the pros and cons for litigants
p1:
What if your courtroom argument was no longer heard only by a judge, lawyers, and those sitting inside the courtroom?

Live-streaming court proceedings is changing how people see justice, but the biggest impact may not be where most people expect.
p2:
Many assume that making hearings public automatically makes the system fairer. The reality is more complicated. Transparency can build trust, but it can also change how litigants behave when their words and emotions are visible to a much wider audience.

A surprising point: even a routine hearing can create lasting digital records that may affect privacy, reputation, and future legal strategies.
p3:
For litigants, live-streaming offers clear benefits: greater public confidence, easier access to proceedings, and a way to see how decisions are made. But it also brings risks, including pressure, unwanted attention, and concerns about sensitive information being exposed.

The key takeaway? Open justice and personal privacy must be carefully balanced. Technology can make courts more accessible, but accessibility should not come at the cost of fairness.
conclusion: Should court proceedings be live-streamed by default, or should the privacy of litigants come first?
